methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $70M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $500M - $32.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5.6B

data.narrative:
Dust is a Series B AI & Software company founded by Gabriel Hubert and Stanislas Polu, having raised $70M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of five same-sector, same-stage private companies (Safe Superintelligence at $32.0B, Physical Intelligence at $5.6B, World Labs at $5.0B, Nominal at $1.0B, and Photoroom at $500M), supplemented by one public comparable, yielding eight private and one public data point.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression modeling, anchored to the $70M total raised as a proxy for post-Series A valuation, applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, implying $133M. Peer-median analysis across the five same-stage AI & Software companies yields a median valuation of $5.0B, with a range spanning $500M to $32.0B. The wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity in business models and investor appetite within the frontier-tech subsector.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $113M (low) to $5.8B (high), with a median of $2.6B. The substantial gap between the stage-progression method and peer median reflects uncertainty around whether Dust's metrics and market position align with frontier-model leaders or with lower-valued applications in the cohort. Key uncertainties include product-market fit validation, revenue traction, and competitive positioning relative to the peer set's varied scale.
